response.justification: Mohammed is a strong senior engineer with compelling, directly relevant experience in building a geospatial data platform (GISCollective) and working on low-latency, high-performance distributed systems. While his professional C++ experience is not recent, his extensive and deep expertise in Dlang is an excellent proxy for the required systems programming skills. His primary weakness is the lack of specific experience in routing or navigation systems, but his strong foundational skills and domain-adjacent experience in GIS make him a high-potential candidate worth interviewing.
strengths: Founded and developed an open-source Geo-Data platform (GISCollective), demonstrating strong, relevant domain expertise.
Extensive experience with low-latency, high-performance systems programming using Dlang, a strong parallel to C++.
Broad technical background and a demonstrated passion for learning and applying the right tool for the job.
concerns: Direct professional experience with C++ is dated (2014-2015), though partially offset by extensive Dlang expertise.
No explicit experience with routing algorithms, tile generation, or navigation guidance solutions mentioned.
The majority of recent experience is with TypeScript/Node.js, not systems-level languages.
